title: Dungeon Crusade - Part Two - Organize It!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=c9CotszUwYQ

Daniel (@dungeondive) follows up his first-look video with a full storage walkthrough — the kind of content he admits some viewers find dull, but which is genuinely necessary for a game this large. Almost nothing fits back in the original box once everything is punched and sleeved.

His solution is a third-party organiser box he already uses for **Warhammer Quest**, **Shadows of Brimstone**, and **Myth**. The main well holds every card deck behind labelled dividers — junk loot, common through legendary loot, lost underworld artifacts, encounter cards, traps, blessings, tavern tasks, minions and champions across all three levels, hero special abilities, plus expansion elite/heroic monster variants. A second tray below stores minion standees with bases pre-attached, gold, three door variants, web tokens, runes of infinity (he sheepishly notes Roger has corrected him from calling them "stones"), initiative tokens for both base and expansion heroes, and combat phase tokens. Smaller bins handle the dozens of quest token types — idols, lanterns, eggs, blood and wine, blacksmith tools, herbs — and a separate bin holds bazaar potions and torches.

He also clarifies his backer history (cancelled the original Kickstarter for unrelated reasons, came back via the pledge manager) and reminds viewers Roger has explicitly said the game will be reprinted with no Kickstarter exclusives — patient buyers can ignore the inflated eBay listings.

----

Is a custom organiser part of *owning* a game this size, or does the need for one signal a publisher should have spent the box budget differently?
